notes: Pre-war baseline. Normal Hormuz traffic ~50+ transits/day.
Day 1: War begins. Initial oil spike. Hormuz warnings issued. Regional airspace disruptions begin.
Day 2: 150+ tankers anchored beyond Hormuz. India activates 'Plan B.' China pivots to methanol.
Day 3: IRGC formally declares Hormuz closed. QatarEnergy halts LNG. 10+ tankers burning. Supertanker rates at all-time highs. KOSPI -12%.
Day 4: AWS data centers hit. Bahrain destabilizing. Supertanker rates all-time high. E3 enters war.
Day 5: P&I insurance cancelled for Gulf. Hormuz down to 2 transits/day. IRIS Dena sunk. Saudi refinery struck.
Day 6: Maersk suspends all Gulf bookings. BAPCO refinery hit. European naval coalition forms. Dollar at 101,650 toman.
Day 7 AM: India 30-day waiver. Phase 2 begins. CSIS estimates $3.7B/100hrs ($891M/day), $3.5B unbudgeted. Quadruple weapons production agreed.
Day 7 PM: WTI spikes to $92. Qatar warns $150/bbl in 2-3 weeks. ALL Gulf force majeure expected 'in next few days.' Qatar airspace partially reopened. 23,000 flights cancelled cumulative.
Biggest weekly oil gain in futures history (+35%). Maritime insurance up 1000%+. 500 tankers stranded. WSJ: $11B/4 days.
